action.description: Whether this entity was newly created or updated via upsert.

id.description: The entity's ULID — use this in subsequent requests. For upserts, this is the existing entity's ID.

label.description: Echoed label if provided in the op, otherwise null.

ref.description: The @local ref from the request — maps back to the original op.

_relationships.description: Relationship summaries with counterpart labels. Capped by rel_limit — use GET /entities/{id}/relationships for the full set.

_relationships_truncated.description: True if more relationships exist than were returned. Use GET /entities/{id}/relationships to paginate through all.

defaults.description: Defaults applied to every op that does not set them individually. Per-op values override.

upsert_on.description: When set to ['label', 'type'], entity ops with matching (label, type) within the same space will update the existing entity instead of creating a duplicate. Requires space_id on the op or in defaults. Entities without a label or space are always created fresh.

ops.description: Ordered list of operations. Entity ops define @local refs; relate ops reference them as source/target. Each @ref must be defined by an earlier entity op in the same list.

oneOf.description: Create a new entity. Any field beyond the reserved keys (op, ref, type, space_id, read_level, write_level, permissions) is stored as a property — inline label, description, and any domain-specific fields directly.

permissions.description: Optional — grant permissions to actors or groups atomically with creation.

read_level.description: Optional read clearance required to see this entity. Defaults to actor's arke default.

ref.description: Local ref for this entity, scoped to this request. Later ops in the same batch can reference it as source/target.

space_id.description: Optional — add this entity to a space atomically. Requires contributor role or above on the space.

write_level.description: Optional write clearance required to edit this entity. Defaults to actor's arke default.

oneOf.description: Create a relationship between two entities. Any field beyond the reserved keys (op, source, target, predicate, space_id, read_level, write_level, permissions) is stored as a property — inline 'span', 'detail', 'confidence', or any other provenance/metadata directly.

predicate.description: Relationship predicate — prefer specific verbs (e.g. 'recruited', 'founded', 'reports_to') over generic ones ('relates_to').

read_level.description: Optional. If omitted, auto-lifts to max(source.read_level, target.read_level).

source.description: Source of the relationship — @local ref or existing ULID.

space_id.description: Optional — add the relationship to a space atomically.

target.description: Target of the relationship — @local ref or existing ULID.

write_level.description: Optional. If omitted, auto-lifts to max(source.write_level, target.write_level).

source.description: Optional provenance — links every created entity back to a source document.

entity_id.description: Source document ULID. Every created entity in this batch will get an 'extracted_from' relationship to this entity. Caller must have read access on it.

extracted_by.description: Optional extraction metadata — e.g. { model: 'gpt-4.1', run_id: '...', at: ISO8601 }. Stored on created entities as provenance.

committed.description: True if writes were persisted, false for dry_run.

created.description: Deprecated — use 'entities' instead. Same array, included for backwards compatibility.

edges.description: Created relationships, with all refs resolved to ULIDs.

entities.description: Ordered list of entities touched by this batch. Each entry maps a @local ref to a ULID (new or existing for upserts). Check the 'action' field to distinguish creates from updates.

score.description: Ranking score (connectivity + recency + proximity + query relevance)

source_depth.description: Hops from the nearest source entity

target_depth.description: Hops from the nearest target entity (null in neighborhood mode)

edges.description: Edges connecting source, target, and discovered nodes

target_ids.description: Resolved target entity IDs (null in neighborhood mode)

truncated.description: True if more results exist beyond the limit

get.description:
Universal graph traversal primitive with two modes:

**Neighborhood mode** (no target): BFS from source, returns top-K ranked nearby nodes.
  Example: `?source=id:01ABC&hops=2&limit=20`

**Bridge mode** (target specified): bidirectional BFS finds nodes connecting two entity sets.
  Example: `?source=properties.work:Moby-Dick&target=properties.work:Crime and Punishment&hops=4`

Source and target accept either `id:<ULID>` for a single entity, or the standard filter syntax
(e.g. `type:concept,properties.work:Moby-Dick`). See GET /help for filter syntax.

Ranking heuristic: connectivity (edge count) + recency + proximity (fewer hops = higher) + optional query-term boost.

parameters.description: Source entity filter. Use id:<ULID> for a single entity, or filter syntax (e.g. type:concept,properties.work:Moby-Dick)
parameters.name: source

schema.description: Source entity filter. Use id:<ULID> for a single entity, or filter syntax (e.g. type:concept,properties.work:Moby-Dick)

parameters.description: Target entity filter (enables bridge mode). Same syntax as source.
parameters.name: target

schema.description: Target entity filter (enables bridge mode). Same syntax as source.

parameters.description: Optional query terms — matching nodes get a ranking boost
parameters.name: q

schema.description: Optional query terms — matching nodes get a ranking boost

parameters.description: Constrain traversal to entities within this space
parameters.name: space_id

schema.description: Constrain traversal to entities within this space

200.description: Traversal result: ranked nodes and connecting edges

x-arke-rules: Source and target entities filtered by your classification clearance
Traversal follows edges in both directions
Limits are global, not per-hop
Results ranked by connectivity + recency + proximity + query relevance
If space_id is provided, traversal is constrained to entities within that space
Bridge mode uses bidirectional BFS for efficiency at higher hop counts

post.description:
Execute a batch of entity and relationship operations in a single atomic transaction.

This is the recommended way to create anything non-trivial. Any time you are creating more than one entity, or an entity plus a relationship, use this endpoint instead of the per-resource endpoints — it is faster, simpler, and atomic.

## Format

The request body is an `arke.ops/v1` envelope containing an ordered `ops` array. Each op is either:

- `{"op": "entity", "ref": "@name", "type": "...", ...inline-properties}` — create a new entity and give it a @local ref so later ops in the same batch can reference it.
- `{"op": "relate", "source": "@a", "target": "@b", "predicate": "...", ...inline-properties}` — create a relationship between two entities. Source and target may each be a @local ref (defined by an earlier entity op in the SAME batch) or a bare ULID (an existing entity).

## Inline properties

Any field on an op beyond the reserved keys is stored as a property on the created entity or relationship. Reserved keys are:
- entity ops: `op, ref, type, space_id, read_level, write_level, permissions`
- relate ops: `op, source, target, predicate, space_id, read_level, write_level, permissions`

Everything else — `label`, `description`, `span`, `detail`, `confidence`, any domain-specific field — flows into properties verbatim. No schema changes needed.

## Ref resolution

- `@jane` — a @local ref. Must be defined by an earlier entity op in this request. Scoped to one call.
- `01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV` — a bare ULID referencing an existing entity.
- `arke:01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV` — same as bare ULID (alternate form).

@local refs DO NOT persist across requests. After this call commits, the response `created` array maps each @ref to its new ULID — use those ULIDs directly in subsequent requests.

## Atomicity

All ops commit together or none do. If any op fails — invalid ref, permission violation, missing target, classification ceiling — the entire batch is rolled back and the response contains an error with `op_index`, `code`, and a `fix` hint telling you exactly what to change.

## Source provenance

Setting `source.entity_id` to a document ULID causes every created entity in this batch to get an `extracted_from` relationship back to that source. Ideal for LLM extraction pipelines — you get full provenance for free. The caller must have read access on the source document.

## Dry run

Add `?dry_run=true` to validate the envelope and return the planned IDs without writing anything. Recommended for LLMs that want to self-check before committing.

parameters.description: If 'true', validate the envelope and return the planned IDs without writing anything.
parameters.name: dry_run

schema.description: If 'true', validate the envelope and return the planned IDs without writing anything.

200.description: Ops executed atomically (or dry-run plan returned)

post.summary: PRIMARY ingestion path — create many entities and relationships atomically in one call. Prefer this over POST /entities + POST /entities/{id}/relationships whenever creating more than one thing.
x-arke-rules: Use this endpoint whenever creating more than one entity, or an entity plus a relationship
All ops execute in a single transaction — all commit or none do
Entity write_level must be <= actor.max_write_level
Entity read_level must be <= actor.max_read_level
Relationship read_level auto-lifts to max(source.read_level, target.read_level)
Relationship write_level auto-lifts to max(source.write_level, target.write_level)
@local refs (e.g. '@jane') exist only within a single request and must be defined by an earlier entity op before being referenced
Bare ULIDs reference existing entities across requests — use these for anything from a previous batch
Any field on an op beyond reserved keys is stored as a property — inline label/description/span/detail/etc. directly
If source.entity_id is set, caller must have read access on that entity and every created entity gets an 'extracted_from' edge to it
Space-scoped ops require contributor role or above on the referenced space
Maximum 2000 ops per request — split larger batches into multiple calls
Only the relationship owner, a system admin, or an actor with edit access on the source entity may delete

post.description: Queues a worker invocation. Returns 202 with an invocation ID by default. Pass ?wait=true to block until the invocation completes (returns 200 with full result). Use the 'then' array to chain sequential follow-up invocations — each step runs only after the previous one completes. With 'then', the response includes a batch_id for polling via GET /workers/batch/{batchId}.

parameters.description: If 'true', block until invocation completes and return the full result. With 'then', waits for the entire pipeline.
parameters.name: wait

schema.description: If 'true', block until invocation completes and return the full result. With 'then', waits for the entire pipeline.

on_fail.description: When a step fails: 'continue' runs the next step, 'cancel' cancels remaining. Only applies with 'then'.

prompt.description: The task prompt for the worker

store_log.description: Override log storage (default: true, respects worker's log_level setting)

then.description: Follow-up steps executed sequentially after the initial invocation completes

store_log.description: Override log storage for this step

worker_id.description: Worker actor ULID for this step

result.description: Structured result output from the worker

invocations.description: All pipeline steps (present when 'then' was used)

post.summary: Invoke a worker, optionally chaining follow-up steps
x-arke-rules: Requires owner, system admin, or invoker permission grant
If 'then' is provided, requires invoke permission on every worker in the chain

get.summary: List permissions on a worker
x-arke-rules: Only the worker owner or a system admin may view permissions

grantee_id.description: Actor or group ULID to grant access to

post.summary: Grant invocation access on a worker (owner/admin only)
x-arke-rules: Only the worker owner or a system admin may grant permissions

delete.summary: Revoke invocation access from an actor or group
x-arke-rules: Only the worker owner or a system admin may revoke permissions

get.description: Returns all invocations in the batch ordered by sequence, with aggregate progress.
get.summary: Get the status of a sequential batch
x-arke-rules: Visibility filtered by RLS — only the batch creator, worker owners, or system admins can see invocations

get.description: Retrieve the current state of a worker invocation. Use this to poll for completion after a 202 response from the invoke endpoint. Includes queue_position when status is 'queued'.

queue_position.description: Position in queue (1-based), null if not queued

get.summary: Get a worker invocation by ID (poll for status)
x-arke-rules: Only the worker owner or a system admin may view invocations

get.description: Retrieves all invocations in the tree rooted at the given invocation ID using a recursive query. Returns a flat array ordered by depth then timestamp, with parent_invocation_id for client-side tree building.
get.summary: Get the full invocation tree from a root invocation
x-arke-rules: Only the worker owner or a system admin may view invocation trees
